title: Machine-readable conformance test vector suite

artifacts.description: Schema documentation: field meanings, how to run vectors, conformance levels
JSON Schema for all vector files — validates input_witnesses, public_signals, expected_result
Happy-path handshake: valid human + agent credential, fresh nonce, correct scope
Agent credential with expiry timestamp in the past — must fail
Human nullifier present in revocation set — must fail
humanMerkleRoot not in the accepted root window — must fail
Delegated credential claims bits not present in parent — must fail
FINANCIAL_MEDIUM set without FINANCIAL_SMALL — must fail validateCumulativeBitEncoding
Two-hop delegation chain — must pass with narrowed scope
Three-hop delegation chain — must pass; scope monotonically narrows
Replayed (humanProof, agentProof) pair with previously-used sessionNonce — must fail
Node script that runs circuit tests with FULL_PROOF=1, captures witness + public signals, and writes frozen JSON vector files
Conformance runner: reads each vector, re-runs witness generation, compares public_signals, asserts expected_result matches actual
AJV-based schema validator — runs over all *.json in test-vectors/ as a pre-commit check

steps: Audit existing circuit tests in circuits/test/ to identify which test cases already produce full witnesses (fast vs slow split); note the 8 scenarios that map to required vectors
Define the canonical JSON schema (schema.json): fields = { id, description, circuit, input_witnesses, public_signals, expected_result: 'pass'|'fail', failure_reason? }
Write extract_vectors.js: wrap snarkjs fullProve for each scenario, capture { proof, publicSignals }, write to spec/test-vectors/<id>.json with frozen values
Run extract_vectors.js with FULL_PROOF=1 against HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, and Delegation circuits to produce the 11 frozen vector files
Hand-author or derive the four negative-path vectors (expired credential, revoked identity, stale root, nonce replay) by mutating valid witness inputs and asserting constraint failure
Write validate_vectors.js: for each vector, call snarkjs.wtns.calculate + check publicSignals match; for expected_result='fail', assert witness generation or proof verification throws
Write validate_schema.js using AJV to gate malformed vector files in CI
Add 'test:vectors' npm script to root package.json: node spec/test-vectors/scripts/validate_schema.js && node spec/test-vectors/scripts/validate_vectors.js
Add README.md explaining schema, conformance levels (L1=schema-valid, L2=witness-match, L3=full-proof-match), and how a third-party SDK can run the suite
Verify all 11 vectors pass validate_vectors.js; confirm 3 negative vectors correctly trigger failures
Commit all files with DCO sign-off; update spec/CONFORMANCE.md to reference the new test-vectors/ directory
dependencies: circom_tester (for witness-only fast path in validate_vectors.js)
ajv (add as dev dependency for schema validation)
FULL_PROOF=1 environment flag for extract_vectors.js — requires ~2min proving time per vector
